prd_coverage.tool_evidence: ls found 24 wideframes covering 8 route screens across desktop/tablet/mobile, and PRD route/screen evidence names the same 8 required screens: dashboard, board, approval, document viewer, trace explorer, task detail, search results, and task list

component_traceability.tool_evidence: grep found 597 data-ds-id occurrences across 48 wireframe files; component-map.json iteration 3 maps 3 user-flow files with 21 unique blocks and 38 unique selectors, including shared shell, dashboard, docs, trace, task detail, and approval surfaces

storyboard_completeness.tool_evidence: storyboards.json is valid and contains 21 trajectories; PRD journey evidence spans dashboard, board, approval, docs, trace, task detail, search, task list, and navigation flows with matching route coverage

layout_compilability.tool_evidence: layout-rules.json parsed successfully and defines desktop/tablet/mobile viewports, global shell rules, shared responsive rules, and complete screen rules for all 8 required screens

conflict_resolution.tool_evidence: prd-ds-conflicts.md documents 2 active conflicts and provides explicit resolution direction for both, plus a normalized create-plan interaction decision; sampled wireframes now use skeletons and pending-bar save language instead of spinner-first wording

wireframe_articulation.tool_evidence: sampled wideframes and tree wireframes show multi-level nesting, selector annotations, and state coverage, but the mandatory mechanical flow check still returned arrows=0 for all 3 user-flow files, triggering FLOW_NOT_CONNECTED and capping this pillar at 20% of max

fix_queue.detail: Repair user-flow ASCII formatting so the mandatory arrow-count check detects connected transitions; all three files still returned arrows=0 in the required mechanical check
